policy.claim_rule: Public claims must not exceed the evidence_level for the owning track.

allowed_claims: Alert inventory is documented from local inspection plus read-only GitHub API checks on 2026-05-14: Dependabot returned 12 fixed and zero open alerts; code scanning returned one open Scorecard alert and 29 fixed Scorecard alerts.
GitHub automation inventory is documented with explicit evidence sources for Renovate, Copilot, Dependabot, CodeQL, Scorecard, branch protection, environments, and GitHub CLI extensions.
Renovate is configured for quiet monthly grouped updates with majors manual and no automerge assignees.
Notification posture avoids scheduled issue creation, broad bot assignment, and default watcher notifications.
Copilot cloud-agent path is prepared with instructions, setup workflow, and security-remediation issue template.
Both docs surfaces (docs/src and docs-site) maintain parity for security-automation content.
blockers: GitHub App installation enumeration was unavailable: user installations returned HTTP 403 for the token type and repository installation returned HTTP 401.

Copilot entitlement activation, installed Marketplace app confirmation, branch-protection changes, environment reviewer/secret verification, labels, and milestones remain GitHub-side settings requiring admin UI access.
No account-side GitHub settings or alerts were modified.

allowed_claims: Registry completion table documents accepted, prepared, deferred, and n/a registries.
Accepted registries have version, URL, date, and install metadata recorded.
Prepared registries have repository metadata with documented blocking requirements.
Deferred registries have blocking requirements and revisit triggers documented.
Package-manager feasibility decisions are documented with rationale.
GHCR evidence is captured as MCP Registry indirection.
server.json and glama.json metadata are reflected in the registry table.
docs/src/release-status.md and docs-site mirror are in parity.
blockers: Public registry listing evidence for Glama requires manual or API verification.
Smithery publication is prepared through track 57 MCPB/local packaging, but no accepted external listing is verified.
Homebrew/Scoop/winget manifests require stable binary release layout.
npm/PyPI launchers require proven MCP client demand.
Automatic verification of registry listings remains excluded from this track.

allowed_claims: server.json is locally validated against Cargo.toml version, repository metadata, stdio transport, and the versioned GHCR OCI target.
Dockerfile and release workflow OCI labels are locally validated against the release version binding.
The MCP registry publishing workflow is locally validated to follow a successful release workflow, wait for the versioned GHCR image, authenticate through GitHub OIDC, and invoke mcp-publisher.
Release-status docs record Official MCP Registry acceptance for 0.1.20 and keep direct GHCR package visibility as prepared until separately verified.
Glama and Smithery distribution states remain separate from Official MCP Registry acceptance.
blockers: No external publication was executed during this track implementation.
Direct GHCR package page visibility still requires separate live verification.
Future version bumps must update server.json, Dockerfile, and release-status docs together or the local regression checks will fail.

allowed_claims: External proof suites are planned and opt-in.
blockers: Fixture and live-smoke evidence required per host.

allowed_claims: Benchmark suite runs deterministically against checked-in fixtures.
Robustness workflow runs on push/PR to main with labelled artifact output.
Stress benchmarks produce labelled metrics artifacts for trend review.
Benchmark claims are explicitly bounded: fixture-backed regression only, no SOTA or external comparability.
blockers: Live-provider throughput or end-to-end latency benchmarks require a separate, opt-in suite with published methodology.
External benchmark suite comparability requires dedicated evidence and a new evidence-ledger entry.
Build-environment dependency (MSVC linker) may prevent local benchmark execution on Windows toolchains.

allowed_claims: Smithery distribution has a prepared MCPB/local package path for the stdio MCP server.
The MCPB manifest template and package builder are checked in and covered by policy tests.
Smithery can be described as prepared, not accepted or publicly listed.
blockers: No Smithery account-side publish was performed.
No accepted Smithery listing has been verified.
Bundle install smoke requires a concrete release binary supplied to scripts/build-smithery-mcpb.ps1.
Streamable HTTP URL publishing remains unavailable until a separate HTTP MCP transport exists.

allowed_claims: Mature Zotero integration is planned.
blockers: Installable package or adapter proof, preview/apply/audit tests, and distribution notes.

allowed_claims: OJS integration has an installable generic-plugin source skeleton that shells out to the Sourceright CLI/MCP core.
OJS screening is fixture-backed with editor-facing and author-facing outputs.
Plugin Gallery readiness is tracked separately from public acceptance.
blockers: PKP Plugin Gallery acceptance is not claimed.
Live OJS handler/settings-form/workflow-template wiring and compatibility testing remain external or future work.
Optional OJS test-instance smoke remains opt-in and not part of default CI.

allowed_claims: No-submodule default and split criteria are formalized in plugin-authoring.md.
Evidence-ledger requirements per status level are documented (fixtures, docs, install path, version, compatibility).
Provenance requirements for installable plugins are documented (signing, pinning, SBOM, deprecation).
Sandbox policy for network/auth/cache permissions is documented and aligned with existing manifest structure.
Status taxonomy and overclaim guards are documented in plugin-authoring.md.
Plugin-registry.md cross-references the authoring guide for packaging and supply-chain policy.
blockers: Live CI enforcement of provenance gates (signed artifacts, SBOM generation) requires separate automation tracks.
Plugin extraction to external repositories is not yet exercised — split criteria remain untested in practice.
Deprecated plugin lifecycle has no real-world examples yet.

allowed_claims: DOCX and PDF adapter-text extraction preserves provenance spans.
Malformed-reference diagnostics queue entries for manual review.
Scanned PDF emits OCR-required diagnostics without fake references.
blockers: True OCR/extractor integration requires adapter loading (track 19).
Column-aware table extraction is not covered by adapter-text fixtures.

allowed_claims: Core academic provider responses are normalized into sidecar-only candidates.
Provider outcomes are classified as match, no_match, ambiguous, or error.
DOI resolution records reachability evidence without bibliographic overwrite.
blockers: Live network tests are opt-in and skip by default.
Provider cache is tested; real rate-limit/retry requires live endpoints.

allowed_claims: Institutional authors and same-author same-year suffixes are handled.
et al. variants and multi-author sequences resolve deterministically.
Mixed-style manuscripts and title-fallback matches produce review issues.
blockers: Non-Latin script author names and rare institutional formats may need additional fixtures.
Unknown formats produce upstream diagnostics, not false matches.

allowed_claims: URL/archive evidence is classified without network calls.
DOI landing pages, redirects, broken, offline, and unchecked states are distinguished.
Canonical CSL is never silently mutated by URL/archive checks.
blockers: Live reachability checking requires opt-in provider configuration.
Archive.org/Memento integration is deferred to future work.

allowed_claims: Writeback suggestions are dry-run by default with explicit apply gates.
Confidence thresholds suppress weak suggestions and route conflicts to review.
Audit logs record all applied writeback actions.
blockers: Zotero API writes require live credentials and are opt-in only.
Schema contract tests validate JSON validity not exhaustive field coverage.

allowed_claims: Dependency automation is configured with grouped, scheduled, safe updates.
Feature contract matrix and design document exist in both docs surfaces.
Live-provider runtime controls default to conservative values.
Copilot cloud-agent support is prepared but requires GitHub entitlement.
blockers: GitHub notification preferences are account-level, not repo-local.
Copilot autofix/merge requires GitHub-side settings and entitlement.

allowed_claims: Overclaim gates exist in conductor/requirements.md, conductor/design.md, and docs/src/limitations.md.
Forbidden claims (production-ready, SOTA, AI-detector, legal-compliance, etc.) are guarded by policy tests.
Evidence ledger enforces claim boundaries per track.
Public docs mirrors maintain parity with Conductor contract.
blockers: Automatic final verification remains excluded until tracks 36-40 and external proof suites pass.
AI detector claims are permanently excluded by policy.
Live provider and registry-accepted claims require opt-in evidence per owning track.

allowed_claims: Catalogue table in docs/src/providers.md lists 25 provider candidates with access model, status, track, evidence level, fixtures, and overclaim guard.
Economics decision log documents deferral rationale for RePEc, SSRN, NBER, EconLit, IDEAS.
Google Scholar is documented as permanently prohibited per ADR 0005 — no manifest, no fixtures, no CI path.
Plugin-registry.md cross-references the expanded catalogue.
blockers: Plugin manifests for planned providers are owned by tracks 48, 49, 50, 58.
Deferred providers (institutional repositories, clinical trial registries) require new tracks for implementation.
No live adapters were added or tested — catalogue is documentation-only.

allowed_claims: GitHub-side governance additions are planned and fixture-backed.
Copilot cloud-agent instructions, security-remediation template, and PR governance checklist are present in the repository.
Branch protection, coverage reporting decision, and release environment protection are documented in security-automation.md.
Renovate configuration is low-noise with monthly grouped PRs; Dependabot PR creation is disabled.
Code scanning surfaces (CodeQL, Scorecard, Dependabot alerts, cargo/npm audits) are tracked.
blockers: Live repository settings observation or documented admin tasks are required for branch protection, environments, labels, milestones, and Copilot entitlement activation.
Live GitHub API or settings modification is required to confirm enforcement state.

allowed_claims: AI client packaging is contracted for Claude, Codex, GitHub Copilot, and generic MCP clients.

GitHub Copilot support is prepared as a coding-agent workflow and remains separate from any Copilot extension claim.
blockers: Client-specific snippets and transcript smoke are still required before client-ready packaging claims.
Copilot entitlement and settings require GitHub-side verification.

allowed_claims: VS Code extension packaging is explicitly deferred with a documented future VSIX contract.

examples/mcp-clients/vscode-mcp.json is MCP client configuration, not a VS Code extension.

blockers: No VSIX package, extension manifest, Marketplace listing, or Open VSX listing exists.

Marketplace/Open VSX acceptance evidence is deferred to Track 69.

allowed_claims: Microsoft Word add-in packaging is explicitly deferred with a documented future Office Add-in contract.

allowed_claims: LibreOffice extension packaging is explicitly deferred with a documented future Writer extension contract.

allowed_claims: Marketplace submission evidence is recorded across MCP directories, AI clients, editor extensions, office add-ins, Zotero, and OJS.

blockers: External listings require URL, version or artifact id, date, install metadata, and verification evidence.
VS Code, Word, and LibreOffice marketplaces remain deferred until package tracks produce installable artifacts.
Zotero .xpi and PKP Plugin Gallery claims remain blocked until package/listing evidence exists.
Private account-side submissions must not be described as completed repo proof until public listing evidence exists.

allowed_claims: All non-main branches are classified as superseded by merged PRs or agent checkpoint artifacts.

blockers: Live branch deletion requires explicit confirmation and review gate.

allowed_claims: CourtListener legal citation provider has src/legal.rs model with LegalProvider::CourtListener enum variant.
courtlistener_fixture_candidate() is publicly exported from lib.rs.
US court neutral citations (SCOTUS, USCA) are mapped to CourtListener with confidence 0.75 by provider_candidates_for_court().
Fixture files exist: fixtures/providers/courtlistener/success.json (1 result, high confidence) and no-match.json (0 results, zero confidence).
Four unit tests exercise CourtListener provider assignment and fixture parsing.
cargo fmt --check passes on src/legal.rs and src/lib.rs.
Plugin manifest plugins/manifests/legal.courtlistener.toml exists with planned_public_api status.
blockers: Earlier Windows MSVC linker failures are superseded by current green CI and GNU-target local verification; no active code-level compilation blocker is recorded for this fixture-backed track.
No live smoke test gated by COURTLISTENER_API_KEY.
No provider-fixtures/courtlistener/ directory for live-provider adapter (only fixture adapter exists).
